UPDATE! I purchased the Amanda white pair of jeans (size 18). I think this size hugs my body more and I like this style. I love the material, but be careful if you have a more curvy hourglass shape like I do. Pants fit very snug around thigh area, but is loose around waist area. I’m 5’7 and 235 lbs. I purchased size 14W plus size Long.

UPDATE! I'm now down around 100 lbs and am in a size 14w! I just ordered another pair in black and cannot wait to receive those too! Ladies, don't hesitate to purchase these jeans! They are comfy and make you feel SO GOOD ABOUT YOURSELF!!! 💖💖💖 I've recently lost almost 90 lbs and although the 18's I ordered appeared to fit fine they felt a little loose through my thighs after an hour so I returned them for a 16. I received those today and wowza! They fit like a glove! These have got to be the most flattering jeans I've ever purchased online! There's the PERFECT amount of flair and stretch and my hubby even loves them on me! For reference I am 5'6" and weigh 210 lbs. Part of me wants to order the size 12 or 14 too since I'm not at my goal weight yet! That's how much I love these jeans! Order them now! You won't regret it!

Update! These absolutely can be cut!! I cut off about three inches so I can wear them with sneakers or flats but they also look cute with heels. A rotary cutter and ruler made it super easy. I ordered them in the black too and they are SO FANTASTIC! They look just like the $100 Abercrombie ones! So happy! Original review: These pants are SO GOOD!!! I am shocked at how perfect these are! They fit perfectly and are so comfortable. They have SOME stretch to them so they are easy to wear for long periods. They will look better with a heel or even sneakers than a bootie, but I was too lazy to change my shoes and socks! :) Some thoughts below: - Follow the size chart for the best fit. I usually wear a womens 6 or 28" waist which can be a small or medium, based on the size chart, I went with the medium and they are SO perfect! I am 5'7" and 135lbs for reference. - It is a faux leather but it does not have that AWFUL fish smell that some do. The material doesn't look super fake either, it looks great. I was SHOCKED. - The inside almost has a lining to it, they are very comfortable and I imagine will keep you nice and warm - I love that it has belt loops and pockets! Neither are super large, but both appreciated. - No butt pockets but I am literally amazed at how good my butt looks in these! I was NOT expecting that! - The ends of the pant legs are unfinished so I THINK you could cut them if they were too long on you. I cannot say for sure as the length is great for me at 5'7" but it appears that you could (see photos) Overall, I am so in love with these pants, I already went back and ordered them in black. I am not kidding when I say I have ordered 10-15 pairs of faux leather pants to find THIS LOOK and I finally found it with these. I am so happy and I cannot recommend them enough!

UPDATED REVIEW: I decided to give it another try and buy another pair in khaki. And the sizing was still not accurate. In the picture, they are both XL that i ordered…but as you can see the khaki is smaller than the black one. I love the style, just wish the sizing was accurate. First review: I first ordered an XL in black. I am 5’4 and 185 lbs. Loved it and it fit great. I ordered a second pair in the mocha brown color in an XL also, but when i tried it on it was smaller than the black one. Really wanted to love these trousers but it’s not great if the XL dimensions are not the same across all the colors. So just be mindful of that when ordering.

Updated review: I returned both sizes. They are just awful. The material seemed like it was nice but any friction, and I mean ANY, like just rubbing your hands on them, cause the fabric to deteriorate. I don't even want to imagine what would happen after washing. Also the butt..it's so sad. Just don't. The pants were much better quality than I expected. Heavy but soft, but not fragile material. I've lost a lot of weight and switch between anywhere from M,L,XL depending on the brand (not weight fluctuatation). I have larger breasts, wide hips and butt. Pros: good quality, comfortable, elastic band and button/zipper, big pockets, high waisted but could be worn lower Cons I saw another reviewer ask if these pants give you "sad butt" and I'm here to say and show the proof.... yes... yes they do give you sad butt. My butt is not happy 😆🤧 Length is kind of weird. I'm 5 foot 4" and they definitely don't fit me cropped even pulled up as high as they'll sit without a belt. They are hitting my ankles and top of feet in a way that evokes memories of childhood pants that had shrunk too short. Conclusion I'm going to order a pair in medium and update. I gave the pants a good review because it's not the pants fault I'm in between sizes and short. And I knew what I was getting into with the "sad butt" which hopefully will be remedied by a smaller size. We shall see.

Updated review.I ordered gray and dark blue. I recieved the dark blue and they are not dark blue at all. They look to me as if these are a purple. A light purple so I contacted amazon bc I really thought that they mistakenly sent the wrong color. So amazon sent me out another pair. I recieved them today and these are exactly the same color as the ones that I still have to return back to them. So just letting people know that they are not dark blue. Would be very cute if they got the dye correct and actually made them dark as shown in the picture. This is not a camera picture color difference. It's a huge difference in color and not even blue a all.. I posted 2 pics of what they say is dark blue. The gray ones are actually gray. Not dark gray but gray. I took 2 stars away bc of this and the annoyance plus having a return them back to UPS for refunds because of their mistake. I like these a lot. They are comfortable and easy to move around in bc they stretch . I've read reviews about broken pockets or rips in them. Mine came in just fine . I have no issues with the ones that I have received. They are not buttlifting at all . I can't remember if they were advertised as being so but they are not. I'm going to be using these as gym pants bc that is what they look and feel like . Oh and all pockets are real usable pockets . I'm 5"5 and these are just at my ankles so not tall girl friendly at all. They are not see thru either. I recommend these as workout / yoga pants or even if you have an operation like I have bc they're not tight around the tummy area .

updated: 4 months in and you can now see the fibers that some reviewers had mentioned. The stretchy fibers are fraying and mine developed in my butt area. I work a desk job so not sure if its because I sit too much or it just happens from stretching over time. Again, only had them 4 months and they're getting to the point that I can't wear them because I'm afraid they'll be more noticeable as time goes on. I only wash them occasionally so its not from over washing. They were the perfect go to work pants up until now :( the pic shows what I'm talking about.... it may not show up on other colors but certainly does on black pants :( yes thats a close up lol but eventually you wont need to be that close to see the light colored fibers against a black background original review: so far so good! they are a little higher waisted than I'd like but thats just a weird pref that I have. They go all the way to my ankles, aren't too tight anywhere, and so far day 3 have held their figure and stretchiness. Haven't washed them yet so we'll see how they hold up. I'm 5'5 and 126 lbs, athletic build and these do the trick! smaller waist but enough stretch for my butt and calves so I'll be ordering more if they hold up!

Upon receipt I washed this top with cool water, gentle cycle and dried it on medium heat. Some of the packaging wrinkles came out but I used a light iron to remove the remaining wrinkles and creases.It is soft and lightweight and has a fairly good stretch. It is well made. I am 5’4”, 150 lbs and the size L fits me perfectly. All of the stitching is straight and unbroken and there are no hanging or loose threads inside or out. The sleeves are a little long but cuffs hold the sleeve in place. They are very well made, even and straight and the gathered stitching is perfect.I love the color and pattern combinations. It is such a fresh, fun style. I look forward to wearing this great shirt for many occasions.

Use the size chart included with the photos of the coat. I'm 5'3" 124 lbs. Size chart said 44 inch chest for the small and that is correct. It is roomy enough for a bulky sweater underneath. Material isn't stiff. Tag has 88% cotton, 12% polyester. Sleeves are a bit too long, so I rolled the cuff up. I think it looks good and will work well.

Use to love these jeans. I ordered the blue jeans in April 2024. Just ordered these last week. I also ordered a blue pair. As you can see, the ones ordered in April fit great lengthwise. Both pairs I ordered last week are at least five inches longer! I’m 5’3 and never had this problem. The zipper also seems cheap. I will be returning both pair. Get your act together on sizing.
Used pants as part of my scarecrow costume. I absolutely Love these pant and got a lot of compliments. I'm 5'4" 162lbs and the med fit perfectly

Used to own the old pair of these in 28 and read reviews to size up so I sized up to a 29 (my waist is 27.5”-28”). The 29 is slightly too big on the waist but a size smaller would have been so tight around the hips and legs. I doubt I would have been able to sit down. If you owned these jeans in the past do not expect the same pair because the fit in the hips and legs is tighter as I mentioned. These jeans have zero stretch but I knew that coming into this. Overall I love these because they are a true high rise unlike other brands’ claims of “high rise” so I will keep them. Don’t worry about the weird “V” shape in my pictures. This happens with all of my jeans because I have very high set hips and I doubt you will have this problem! I am 5’7” and 128lbs with a short torso.

Usually I don't go for high wasted pants; these definitely have some mom-jeans energy, but that's more of a style preference thing. They do fit fine, and button over my belly button. I'm 5'7", 135 lbs, and wear size sm/size 4. They are comfortable and don't pinch anywhere. They're a bit stinky. I washed them with like-colors, and they definitely lost some of their color, and still carry their manufacturing odor. It's no worse than the smell of new Levi's, and I'm hoping it'll be gone after a few more washes. I'm a bit sensitive to that kind of thing. The fabric is comfy. It feels like real jeans, but has a lot of elasticity which makes movement really easy. They're a bit plain for my usual style, but aside from the odor, they seem to be well-made, and a reasonable style. If you love the high-waisted look, I think you'll be pleased with these. They're a reasonable value, and very comfortable.

Usually I’ll buy some fashion nova jeans in a 15 for $30 and they’ll show up a little tight but stretch out in a few hours. Few months in they’ll tear at the thigh anti-gap lol. A coworker convinced me to get jeans at Target in July. Got 3 pairs of “Levi’s” in a 14 (because they’re stretchy) for like $40/ea. Literally wearing these things to work every day because that’s the only thing I can do through this apocalypse. Some how a pair of these garbage pants ripped in a month. A MONTH. So. I’m on amazon and I see these here bad boys on sale for $9 and I figure I should honestly just buy six pairs and call it a night. Just in case though I upped it to a size 16. If they run small, they’ll fit. If they run regular, I’ll wear a belt. Well guess what. They run regular. The delivery lady Just dropped them off and I pulled one on and these bad boys are so comfortable, but I ordered them big lol. Anyway, I’ll wear a belt to work. I got two pairs but I came back to buy two more, but the sale is over, sad day, but not that sad. They seem way sturdier than the ones on fashion nova AND these pants not only have 4 1/2 Real Pockets but I can also fit my entire hand in the front ones. Heck, I can fit a couple TV remotes in the front pockets. I’m definitely coming back, and even though the $9 sale is over, full price is still a steal compared to Target’s “Levi’s” For reference I’m about 5’5 and 197lbs this week but I’m kindof dense in the bones. A lot of my weight is butt, a lot of it is thighs...these jeans don’t quite do the butt justice but that’s definitely on me because I purposely ordered them too big haha if I figure out how to update this review, I’ll edit to add pictures when I buy the right size

Usually, I wouldn't say I like wearing jeans because I find them uncomfortable after a while. But KHAKI & BLUE Cali1850 Women's The Everyday Skinny Jeans are super comfortable and soft. I usually wear a size 4, but based on the sizing guide, I needed a 6. I'm 5'7 and weigh 120 lbs, but my hip width fits the size 6. The jeans are bigger than I'd like, looking a bit baggy when they should be a slim fit. Overall, they are a great causal jeans.

Versatile, comfortable, perfect length. I ordered 4 pair and they seem to fit well. The back shows the panty line a bit but wearing different underwear or longer tops could take care of that issue, they're not thin/ see through. I have the 29 inch in seam in a Large & I'm 5'2 ~160 lbs. I don't care much for jeans so these were a good option for me to wear to work, church or casually. Haven't washed them yet so I can't speak to that but I wore them all day & had zero issues.
Very classy, soft, beautiful color. I’m 5’4” and 100lbs, size S is slightly loose but a very comfy fit! I ordered more colors. They look even better with heels on.

Very comfortable and a cute fit. I’m always worried about buying pants like this as sometimes there a bit tonight on the thigh's, these are not! They fit perfect in the thighs and buttocks area. However, I do have a small waste, and they left a bit of an indent in the skin. I’m 5”8 130 lbs. I ordered a small and they are very comfortable I bought this with The Drop razor back rib shirt that was on sale. Total cost less than 50$!
Very comfortable and fit great... But... Just too short... And they don't get skinny at the ankle so I looks really weird. I'm 5'6" and it's a no.
Very comfortable and fit true to size Not excessively long so didn't require any alteration for me at 5'6 wearing sneakers
Very comfortable and fits as expected. I’m 5’4”, 120lbs. Beautiful, bright color as well.
Very comfortable and forgiving. For reference, I ordered size L. I'm 5'3" 171 waist 34.5" hip 41.6". Since I am short, they fit as a regular length jean. (vs. ankle)

Very comfortable and great quality. Thick enough for cooler weather. Not see through at all. Runs true to size. Very stretchy. Pants are short girl approved. I’m 5’3 and usually get a 30 inch inseam. 10/10 recommend!

Very comfortable and looks flattering on me. I am 5'3" tall and my weight is142 lbs. Size 6 fits me perfectly. Fabric is thin but stretchable. I am giving 4 stars because of the color. I ordered cape town color. Just got it and the color is nothing like online picture. I already have the color I got so undecided what to do.

Very nice looking pants, very dressy and professional. I ordered the beige. The word says beige, but the picture looks more like a very light sand, almost white. They are as pictured. I was actually hoping for something a little more beige because I’ve never had much luck with virtually white pants. They all seem to stain so easy. The first time I wear them they come home with an ink spot, coffee stain, or something. These did not! I was very impressed. I wore them to the office on a regular workday. I took the train into the city. Wore the pants all day. When I came home, they looked great. A few wrinkles around the hip flexors, which is normal for an office job. I was eating a breakfast bar at my desk and dropped a few chocolate chips on the pants so I went into the bathroom and just wiped them off with a wet paper towel and the chocolate came right off. It’s great to be able to have such a light colored pair of pants that don’t stain easy. And they’re so easy to care for. They were a little creased out of the package, as you can see by some of the photos. I tried them on right away. I was wearing a black and purple knee brace and I just happened to have on black underwear. Could not see through them at all, which can be a problem with such light colored pants quite often, and they fit perfect. I touched them up with a steam iron and it came out fine. Care instructions. The tag inside the pants list just the care instructions symbols, and I didn’t know what they all meant, but there is a card attached to the pants that translates them. You might want to save the little card, that’s what I did. Care instructions for these pants translate to: Do not bleach, dry clean, Machine wash up to 86°F general cycle. iron up to 230°F tumble dry low. So you can dry clean them and they’re very nice pants. They’re worth it if you want to. But it also says machine wash up to 68°F gentle cycle, tumble dry low, iron up to 230°F. So I threw them in the washer by themselves on delicate, and then threw them in the dryer on low. And I am very impressed. They’re clean and they came out of the dryer virtually wrinkle free. So I’m not gonna bother to test iron them, they don’t need it. Very easy to care for. Sized as stated. I measured myself and bought the one according to the measurement chart, and it fit perfectly. May be too short for average to tall people. The one thing the measurement chart does not say is the inseam. This concerned me because I’m 5’4”” with a 28 inch inseam, and very long waisted. These pants fit me perfectly, I can wear them with flats. They come up to my waist. I measured the extra large inseam is 29 inches so it’s perfect for me. However, as my sister says, for anyone who actually has legs, lol, they may end up, looking like high water pants. But from my short legs, they’re perfect. I’m thrilled with the inseam.

These pants fit me perfectly, I can wear them with flats. They come up to my waist. I measured the extra large inseam is 29 inches so it’s perfect for me. However, as my sister says, for anyone who actually has legs, lol, they may end up, looking like high water pants. But from my short legs, they’re perfect. I’m thrilled with the inseam.

For reference, my daughter has an athletic build. She is almost 5'4, and 132 lbs. She wears a size 7 in juniors. I hope this gives you a good idea for sizing. It's a very nice black, real leather, belt. It's not too thin, or too wide. I imagine it will fit any belt loop size, but still has a nice wide width. I will post pics of the belt when I get home. I would definitely, reccomend this, and will probably purchase in other colors when available.

Vine reviewer here giving you my honest hands-on review and inspection of the product you are considering. I ordered a small, I wear a 10 and it has some room for me to move, but too tight for me and the look I want. I am losing weight, so I plan on fitting into this by winter. So, if you are an 8, a small or medium will probably fit nicely, depending how loose you want it. At a size 10, I should have ordered a medium or large. I feel the sizing chart is accurate. The color is dark, dark blue, as is on the image online. My pics lighten the color a lot. It is long and comes past my fingertips. I am short, 5'2", so a tall person will still get that tunic look. It is made with a thin, tightly woven, quality knit fabric: very stretchy and super comfy. Even with the tunic being a little too tight, the fabric was forgiving and not clingy. The crew neck is just the right size. It doesn't come up too high, making your neck look short. The sleeves are just a little different. They are in two pieces. This works. They are snug, yet still very comfortale. You can bunch them and the stay. I like them a lot. Of course, the pockets in the front are a big win, and they lay flat and don't add bulk. Overall, a nice knit tunic.

We love these pants! The glitter is very fine, so it’s not prickly! The here is great stretch, but I suggest sizing up if you are curvy and/or tall. We originally ordered a large, but the XL gave us the length we needed and the pants weren’t hugging the thighs. 5’ 6.5” and 160 pounds.

We ordered for my 16 year old daughter that typically wears a size 9-10. She has athletic thighs so we did get a size bigger and I'm glad we did. Fit at waist was great in a 12, but had we gone smaller I think thighs would have been too small. She is 5'6" and the length was perfect for her to wear with western boots with small heel. Bottom hems are unfinished so they could easily be taken up if needed. The material was VERY stretchy. After first was there was no bleeding of fabric (I ordered light wash color) and seams did not unravel. I personally think she looked great, she was comfortable all day at school and all evening in these and says she will gladly wear them with everyday items and not just as part of a costume. Super pleased!

We ordered this faux leather belt from QiqiLuxi and it was priced at $10 at the time. The quality of the fake leather is about average, your basic inexpensive material. The buckle and belt tip where actually heavier duty than I expected and look pretty good. The belt material, 3 stars, the simple inexpensive buckle, 5 stars. The posting itself seems misleading as this is a womens belt, it is much to thin to be considered a mens belt. The size also suggests it is good for up to 38" waists... the limit is closer it 33". The miss has 38" hips and no way this will work low low cut jeans, it was in the furtherst hole with a set of very high waisted jeans (on the miss with a 32" waist). I have 4 stars as the buckle and belt cap are nice for the price, but it could of got 3 stars as the belt itself is cheep material and shorter than expected.

Well... kinda on the fence... I went with the Size: X-Large | Color: Khaki. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. These pants have a lot of orange/pink to them for being "khaki" depending on the lighting. But we all know khaki is one of those colors we all have different ideas of what it actually is. This fabric is very light weight, flowy, with some small stretch and they kinda look like a linen. They are 100% polyester though. That means they haven't shrunk or anything in the wash and they don't hold the wrinkles nearly as badly as linen does. But that also means they don't breath as well either. On the softness scale of 1-5, 5 being super soft, these fall around a 4. They are so light that they aren't often in contact with your skin unless you're sitting, but the texture has not bothered my sensitive skin at all. The stitching all seems fine, decent construction. The pockets are a nice feature. Maybe it's because of the size, but the front and back are not that different. And on me, it's unfortunate where some of the pleats fall from the elastic waist. It adds to the look of my belly. Not something I need exaggerated. With a shirt over it, it can look a little better but I will probably alter the waist line in time. Overall, they are pretty decent pants that would work in an office or for traveling. With the price being under $20, that bumps these an extra star. I would say they are worth checking out. Peace and comfy pants

Welp, here I am again. Wrote a similar review on a similar pair of wide leg yoga pants only a couple weeks ago. Sigh! Here’s the verdict- these are overall cute yoga pants with good options on color and inseam length. They could really use a size XS though, the size small was just a touch big for me (jeans size 24/25 waist). My real gripe here is the lack of description of the waistband and the actual waistband. I hate HATE having the little elastic band at the top of the waist in leggings/yoga pants. It is not comfy, not flattering. I want a nice wide seamless band that lays flat. I read every word of this item’s description and could not find a clear answer. I then read all the reviews, fruitless venture. I then posted my question to the Amazon community, fruitless venture. Answers were literally like 4 people said no, doesn’t have the elastic and 2 said yes. Baffled by this, I decided to just give them a shot on a lightning deal sale. They arrived today and are going back. I decided to post this review to help anyone who shares the strong hatred of skinny elastic and I decided to include a photo to help show the Amazon community what it is. (I should note that I bought the khaki color and because this is Amazon I cannot rule out the possibility that some colors may have it and others may not. I will not be purchasing 8 pairs in order to find out).

Welp. Here I am trying to style a 90s look with a more modern feel as a 45 year old. I went with my TTS 25 and think the more fitted waist works with the oversized legs. Still unsure if I am keeping these jeans as this cropped wide shirt is the only thing I currently like with them. If you can come up with more tops to go with the jeans I say buy them.

What a great Lululemon On the Fly Dupe!!! I wear a 6 in this style of pants and ordered a M (the tag says 8-10 when they came). They fit very well! WHAT ARE YOU WAITING FOR? GO BUY THIS JACKET!! I have not been paid or given a free product for this review. I just have a hard time finding clothes that fit good so I’m super excited about this purchase lol First thing I look for.. is this person shaped like me? So here it goes.. I ordered a large. I’m 5’1, 165lbs, 38DD or 36DDD, I guess I’m considered an hourglass shape ? My bust and hips are pretty much the same width and my waist is a few inches smaller. I carry my weight pretty evenly. Typically, I love how jean jackets look and hate how they fit so I was really skeptical and was pretty sure it would be returned but now I wish I could afford one in every color but I’m a single parent with 3 kids so mom splurges are limited but seriously... this jacket is amazing!!! So, let’s dive in to this review... THE MATERIAL AND FIT I’m the past, my experience with jean jackets has been awful. The material is thick, stiff, heavy, unyielding.. just not comfortable at all and having a large bust with a stiff jacket is seriously unflattering. If it fit my boobs, then it hung like a box at my waist. No definition at all. Think potato sack/tent... you get the picture 😂 NOT THIS JACKET!! This jacket has spandex or something in it! Not only does it fit my bust line but it curves in towards my waist beneath it. It is PERFECT and the material is a soft, thinner denim, but not thin like a shirt, it’s thick enough to function as a jacket and still keep you warm but soft and thin enough that is shapes to you and hugs your body... think, your favorite pair of jeans. That is how this jacket feels! I purchased a large, I’m a crazy in between size. I wear a 12 pants in women’s, 13/15 pants in juniors and my tops range from M-L-XL depending on the cut, brand, where it’s made. My waist screams “medium“ but my bust yells back “large” and if it’s not US sized... my whole body starts begging, “for the love of God woman get the XL... and don’t be scared of the 2X if it’s coming from China!” The large in this jacket was perfect. An XL would have been to big and a medium to open in the front, but I will be able to look great in this even if I lose 10-15 pounds (which I’m currently working on) I was diagnosed with Lupus two years ago and the medications caused me to gain a ton of weight so I’m happily 60lbs lighter since this past November and finally starting to see my body again! The fit of this jacket is AMAZING! It hangs with perfect spacing in the front if it’s unbuttoned. It stretches enough that I can button it up if I want, might be a tiny snug over the bust but the stretch is fantastic... and let’s face it, I’m not really going to button this thing. Does anyone really button up a denim jacket? I noticed some reviewers saying the arms were long and there was no pocket space. I didn’t have an issue with either and I’m short y’all! With my arms down, the cuff comes to the top of my fingers and if I raise my arm straight up over my head, it falls at the perfect wrist spot for the sleeves being down (but y’all know I’m going to cuff them anyway, I mean come on... it’s cuter that way lol) I also found there to be sufficient room in the pocket. My hand is completely open and stretched in the photo and fits. You could easily put your keys, gum or hands in the pocket... cell phone? No, but that’s not the jackets fault. Cell phones are ridiculously big and barely fit in our BACK pocket these days. Overall, my review is a YES, YES, YES.. I’ll have what she’s having in every single color... at least, as soon as my pocket book says I can lol !! (Little Harry Met Sally reference for you there 😉 yeah... I’m that old lol) So stop guessing, stop staring at it, stop debating... GO BUY THIS JACKET!! You will 💕💕💕💕 it!

They do not feel durable and there were loose threads all over them. Not worth it at all for me. Maybe you’ll have better luck.

When I find a pair of jeans (or pants in general) that fit, they are usually baggy in the tummy area and fit my butt (or vice-versa) and 9 times out of 10 they are way too long so I end up taking them in to be hemmed. I love wide leg pants and usually I guess they aren't the most flattering on shorter legged people - I am - 36-33-42 and 5'4" - and maybe I'm just kidding myself but I think these jeans look pretty good. And even better, they feel GREAT on. They have a perfect stretch. They fit my butt but aren't too tight - I always have trouble there too. I washed them with darks and tumble dried them and they came out great. The unfinished hem still looks good - no extra fraying or anything like that while being laundered. I think they are fabulous jeans.

When I first got these I was so impressed. I got 1 day in them and the second day the zipper broke and was not repairable. I have requested a return for the item and will try a replacement just because I love the style and so many compliments. As far as sizing, pretty much as expected. I am 5’9, 165 lbs and ordered a large, I could probably even go down to a medium. I Just wish that the materials used were better quality. I will update when I get my replacement and see how they withstand time.

When I first got these jeans I liked the cut and fit around the waist and they were comfortable to sit in and did not gap at the waist. What I did not like when I first got them was the length; I am 5’ 11” and ordered a long size, however the cuff was still a couple inches above my ankle. Not the length I was hoping for but I kept them because I liked the waist fit well enough. Having worn them for a couple weeks I’m really not a fan of these jeans. The material snags easily and little loops have pulled up all over my lap from my cat laying on me. She’s not scratching me or doing anything crazy, but if a claw is the least bit out then it will snag and pull up the jeans. None of my other jeans have ever had this happen to them so I am confident it’s something about the material of these jeans and not a flaw with all jeans.

When I first got these jeans, I loved them! They are way longer than anticipated. I am 5’8” and they were dragging the floor. The jeans themselves have good stretch but the waistband is a little tough. The button actually popped off though and the zipper feels very cheap. I’ll be shopping for a replacement button.

When I first opened these I was really excited because the fabric felt really comfortable, flowy, and a good weight. After trying on, I didn't like the fit though. I'm 5'7" and I didn't like that when I wore these up high on my waist like the models that the ankle elastic hit a couple inches above my feet. I could pull the waist down but the waist is a very wide elastic so I didn't find that flattering or where I wanted these to sit anyway. Also, the ankle elastic was the worst part of these pants. Everything else was extremely comfortable so it made the ankle elastic stiffness stick out obviously. It felt simple and cheap and a little itchy.

When I first opened these jeans, the "raw hem" was not super noticeable. After a single wash, the amount of fraying at the hem did not bode well for the longevity of these jeans. I suspect each wash will get a heavier and heavier amount of fraying. If you like a cleaner look, you can trim them up again. I intend to wear them with sandals and the strings tickle my feet, so I will trim them up. They are marketed as cropped jeans, but at 5'3" with short legs, they are really just regular jeans for me. They fit well and are comfortable. The large flat pockets on the back are not the most flattering as they make your bottom look droopy. I sized up from what the listing recommended. I usually wear a 4 or 6, but do not like my jeans tight. I am happy that I did. I have a bit of a gap at the back around the waist, but these fit appropriately through the hips and thighs. Overall, I like the fit, but the quality is questionable.

When I ordered this skirt I was skeptical about the length as it is shown as a mini on the young model. I am a short 5ft senior and it is a perfect little casual skirt for me. Very comfortable fit being mostly cotton and spandex. Just washed in cold and hung to dry.

When I placed the order, the size 4 long was not available and I just ordered the four regular thinking the length would be long enough on me. For reference I'm 5'4" about 175/180lbs, waist 35". XL was perfect. Fits just like my comfy linen pants.

when I received them I put them on immediately and will probably wear them for the rest of the week! I am 5’ 2 and a half, and the small fits perfectly. When I saw these jeans they reminded me of a pair I had back in my day as a teen, I am 56 years old and I still love fashion so I had to have this pair. I am so glad I got them...these jeans are true to size and they are well made. I love this darker color (they come in different colors), the pockets are very cool being different of either side, the front and back pockets are nicely sized and the waist is high, very comfortable and I love the flare cut which is perfect for any sandals, boots, heels or booties. These jeans are my new favorite pair and frankly the best pair of Jeans in my closet at the moment. 10 OUT OF 10!!!

They're a little long but they look so cute rolled up and with heels. They don't have a lot of stretch but the denim is great quality and fits so nice. Wearing a 6 5'2" 140ish lbs 27" waist 42"hip

When I took these out of the package they looked too long, but they are very high-waisted, so at 5'4" they were fine - maybe a smidge long - but as they have an opening in the front they work well. They are not as loose and flowy as the photograph. As you can see they are more like a loose-fit straight leg than the very-full-flowy fit in the picture. But they are soft and comfortable and will work around the house and running light errands. I wear a 10 and ordered a medium which fit well.

When I took these sweatpants out of the packaging, the first thing I noticed was their weight. These pants have a substantial feel to them, which is a sign of better quality compared to other sweatpants from Amazon that feel flimsy and are made from cheap material. These sweatpants look exactly as pictured and fit me very well. I am 5’4 and usually wear an inseam of 28-29 inches and these pants just hit my heels without touching the floor. There are no issues with the stitching or cut, unlike other sweatpants that are often cut crooked, fit oddly, or have seams coming apart. While these pants are on the thinner side, the material is not see-through. The inside isn't as soft as some other sweatpants, but it's still comfortable. The only major complaint I have is the design on the front. The item description calls it "elevated seams," which appear subtle and neat in the picture. However, in reality, the seam is crooked and doesn't look good. I don't think ironing would fix this issue, and it would be a hassle to iron sweatpants every time you wear them. I have another pair with this design that looks much better and doesn't have this problem, as shown in my pictures.

When it arrived, the material was soft, like well worn denim. The fit was great, just roomy enough, not so tight that you can’t bend over. Quite a bit too long, but at 5’2”, I’m used to that. (The picture is before washing.) Then I washed them. Instructions said ‘cold water, do NOT tumble dry’, so that’s what I did. Yikes. I’d say they shrank somewhere between 1/2 to a full size. They ended up extremely wrinkled and the material became super stiff and rough, like the denim my Grandpa used to wear. I will try washing again and this time putting in the dryer; will update if they come out any better. Or worse.

When it comes to clothes, its hard to know exactly what you will get if its not brands that you regularly buy, so I got these jeans with a small amount of hesitation. I was so wrong to worry, they are fantastic. I love the fit, they have some stretch to them, but not the kind that makes them look sloppy or too big, these are very fitted in a good way. The length is perfect for me and the rise is good. I normally only buy Lucky or Banana Republic jeans, so to get these was a risk, but honestly these are great. for reference, I am 5'4", 125lbs and normally wear a small 6 or roomy 4. I got these in a 4 because the description said they are stretchy and they fit perfectly.

When my husband and I sold everything we owned & moved to Mexico, I packed on almost 30 lbs. We overindulged in all the delicious food here, and stayed in Airbnbs for months with no access to a gym. As a result, none of my clothes fit anymore, so I needed something I could wear that was at least as flattering as possible given the current state of my body. Since my size 10 jeans were too tight, I decided to get a 12 in these. In the photos, I weighed about 180 lbs, 37 waist , 42.5 hips and they were the tiniest bit snug, but the spandex in the denim allowed me to confirm to my body instead of giving me muffin top. Since I'm 5'7" with long legs, I ordered the 32 inch inseam. The length is perfect for a low wedge heel on me. I love that these jeans are lightweight with some stretch. So much better than the stiff denim from the old days (I was born in the 70s, raised in the 80s). The denim back then was thick and so stiff if you gained an ounce jeans weren't wearable anymore, and even if you could squeeze into them, they dug into your skin and left indentions and red marks. This more modern, stretch denim is so much more comfortable to wear, and the lighter weight fabric is more bearable in hot climates like Mexico.

When ordering these for my daughter, I didn't even realize that they had these really cute tie detail at the back . After receiving them, they are even cuter than anticipated and my daughter loves them. For reference, she is 5'0, so I wouldn't think they would work for taller people, but they are perfect for her. I wouldn't hesitate to order them again.

When reading the reviews, I was a little confused by the sizing, but thanks to the reviews and pics with similar body types to mine, I found the perfect size! These are actually junior sized. For reference, I am 5’7” 136 lbs and I ordered a size 7. I am a size 4 in womens shorts. They fit great and have a nice stretch! I’ll be ordering more colors! Hope this review helps someone!

Where can I start? As a shorter person 5'3" I have major difficulties finding jeans that don't hang many inches below my feet. Not these gems from Aeropostale who offer lengths in short, regular and tall! That's something few jean companies do but all should. The color is that perfect medium/dark wash blue! And the quality is off the charts! You can tell these jeans will be a staple in your closet for years to come. These midrise jeans bring me back to my youth in the 90's when big (within reason) was in. They are super comfortable and at the sale price of $23 they are a steal! So order a pair or 3. They are super versatile and can be worn with a hoodie and sneakers or a blazer and heels. The one complaint I have is the slight gap in the back of the waist. But as someone with a naturally smaller waist and a bigger booty, this is an issue I frequently encounter. It can usually be helped with a good quality belt. All in all a great pair of jeans from a great company!
Where do I start! I’m 5’4 and this is the FIRST TIME I’ve ordered jeans and the fit was PERFECT!!!🙌🏾🥰 So comfortable and do not have to hem them!!!

While I like the style and fabric, sadly these jeans are more for a pear-shaped woman and I am apple shaped! They have a very generous fit through the thigh and hip area and they are too long for me. I am 5'3-1/2" and weigh 79kg. Size 22 Plus Short was way too large!

While I really do like this set and the quality was not an issue, it just didn’t fit my frame/body very well. I’m 5’8” tall and 125 lbs. the pants were the biggest problem. They weren’t long enough and the crotch was way, way too long. Even rolling down the waistband they were still too long in the crotch and became much too short in the overall length. I just don’t think this set was designed for taller, thinner gals. It’s a shame because I’d love to have kept it but had to return.

While I'll show my age by telling you I used to get 501s for 20$ back in the day, I've always loved them and these cute ones didn't disappoint. At 5'4" 123 I got a 28 regular and they fit well. Slightly loose but after washing 100% cotton I'm sure they'll be perfect. Life's short, buy the jeans :)

While Inread the reviews and most people have issues with the gems coming off, I do not. My issue is with the length. They are so long you can’t even fashionably roll them up. Now I’m very short (5’1) so unless I wear a platform shoe, these are too long by a solid 5”. If you have longer legs it may not be an issue for you. I’m 5’1 195 typically size 12 these are a large and fit comfortably

While these are very comfortable and fit nicely, I did find a rip along the outside seam after wearing only once. After looking at the seams closely, I can see that it is not sewn with strong thread and will have the propensity to come undone with little pressure. I only wore the pants for an hour and had been sitting in them when I noticed the rip along the seam. I'm going to have a tailor fix the rip and reinforce the seams as I really do like the look and the fit of the pants. Other than this problem, I love them. They wash well and the material has just the right amount of stretch. Being that I only have worn them for an hour, I can't say whether they stretch out more than they should over the hours of wearing them, but after I get them fixed, I will update the review with how they hold up. I wash them in cold, gentle cycle, using Woolite Dark (since they are black jeans). I then lay them to dry. While I really do like the pants, I'd probably think twice about purchasing this brand again only because of the ripped seam. I am 5'4" and 155 pounds. The size 10 short fit nicely and came down to just below my ankle. I really like the panel in the front of the jeans that help keep my tummy in a bit (see picture).

While they are very soft and stretchy they are on the longer side. I am 5'2" and 3/4 and as you can see they do not come up to where they should be to technically be considered capri's. So short people beware! 🙄

While they did "fit" as expected, the styling is sloppy and unflattering for me. I have extra weight on my upper thigh and these pants highlight that. Also seemed to be baggy and slouchy in the legs at the bottom. Long length was perfect and I'm 5' 7" but leg is too tapered. Very comfortable and moving around and bending was easy. Extra material in crotch area isn't flattering. With all that said, I'm keeping them to wear to my "khakis required" workplace (corporate America please stop this!) and I will vow to avoid mirrors. The sloppy cut to these seems to be lessened when paired with a company polo.
While they were long at first, I threw them in dryer for one cycle on low heat and now they’re the perfect length (I’m 5’1)

White pair Little lose but so comfortable, I weigh 169 and 5’5. I freakin love these. I will most definitely be ordering more. Fav for sure! They are high waisted witch I like because I have a mommy tummy. Y’all seriously these are worth it. The Jean ones are my all time fav they fit great obsessed with them there so flattering. I am definitely not disappointed so satisfied.

Who buys jeans on Amazon and actually loves them ???? I barely can find a pair in the stores that I like after I try in 20+ !!! These jeans are light weight and super comfortable. I haven’t found a flare bottom bell that I really love yet so I’m not knocking these … just not sure on the bottom shape yet. The length was perfect for me! (… but I’m 5’2 and didn’t order a short … so keep that in mind ) .. I’ll follow up after I wear them a full day and wash them a few times…. But first impression is 10/10

WHO LIKES SWANK JEANS??? These are the same! The fit, color, material. I bought navy blue in XL and wore them a few times over a month. I then ordered 4 more pairs in the following sizes and colors... black and olive in XL. I also tried the burgundy and red in the XL PLUS since they were only $10 bucks at the time. I'm 5'4 and currently 175 with an authentic round shaped peach 🍑, thick. My weight does fluctuate down to 160 at times. I see no difference AT ALL in the XL and XL plus, exact measurements, IMO. I prefer that my pants hug my thighs, but I do not like the button and zipper to fit tight to the point that it's squeezing my midsection & I feel uncomfortable. These are a perfect fit around the waist area. I'll get a large as my weight continues to drop back down. Update: The photo is of me in gray pants because my sister couldn't fit what she orders in XL- plus so she gave them to me.

Who would have thought—Lee makes great black work slacks!! I am 175lb and 5’6” and the 14 Medium fit great! They are a perfect length to wear with my clogs. They are stretchy but also firm/not baggy and look like slacks and not yoga pants. I ordered about 10 different pairs of “wide leg black womens pants with pockets” and for some reason, all you get are “yoga work pants” which are too thin and too casual for this traditional gal… I want comfy pants but I also want them to be more like slacks, and not hug my butt so much!! (All the wide-leg “yoga pants” are too tight around the butt…) These are a little on the tight side but I think they will stretch out as I wear them. Winners!!!

Who: purchased for myself. 6’1” Tall, 175lbs. Pear shape build. I am a size 12, so I went with the size 13. I could hardly get the button done. Was holding my breath as I zipped them up. Gave me the worst looking pooch and flattened any butt I had. Extra fabric in the crotch, thighs and hips. NOT flattering at all and NOT a skinny jean. Singular wash so not all that much to look at (could be cut, bleached or altered to your liking though). This is where the term “Mom Jeans” originated. RETURNED. What: 36” Inseam Blue Jeans for Women. No smell, good quality. When: arrived in a timely manner (used 2-day Prime) Why: needed LONG jeans. Overall: not happy with purchase. Fit horribly on my body.

Whoa! This is a NICE belt!! It's got an easy to see detailed design, and the embossed design on the buckle is stunning! Also, this is one of those soft belts that conforms well to the body and does not dig into my hip bone. I wore this belt all day on the day I received it and it stayed comfortable throughout the day. Also very happy that it would be easy to change out the buckle if I wanted, just 2 snaps and your can remove the buckle to replace with another.For reference, I am 5'6" and weigh 111 lbs. I usually wear somewhere between 4-6 depending on the pant, so following the size chart in this listing and ordering the 31-33 gave a great fit. There are a couple more notches if I ever need them so there is some leeway on the fit. As of now, this is without question the most comfortable belt I own. I have no hesitation in recommending this 5-star belt!
Why does everyone think a plus size means the person is tall....?? Not all size 16+ people are 5'6"+...!!!

Wide leg jeans are back in style! I love the color and stretch of these jeans. The back pockets are placed in a good location. The zipper and button are durable. My hips are 44” and accidentally ordered two sizes down, a size 10 instead of size 12. I was able to get these jeans on and since I’ve lost over 35 pounds this year, I will save these for my next goal weight, when I will fit into size 12 clothes at 195lbs. The inseam length is rather long and you can see I have on 3” platform shoes to compensate. You could raw cut the hems or actually sew a hem if your inseam is short like me at 29”. These jeans are cute and can’t wait to wear them more.

Wide leg linen blend pants from Sevego. There are 2 pockets in the front, high waist with elastic and a faux button. These come in 5 sizes, small to xx-large and 3 length options; 29", 31" and 33" inseam. The beige pants are a little sheer and would look better with slip shorts underneath. Great for Spring and Summer. A good value.

Wife is 5'1" weights 102. Extra small fit perfect. PS not bad for 56 years old.

Wife is a size 8, 29" waist, 40" hips, 5'5", 150lbs. These pants are awesome. They look professional but the material is super stretchy and easy to wear. It's not quite the same material as leggings and definitely feels more like business wear, but the stretchiness is next level! Very flattering for the figure and still easy to wear every day.
